observations.recommendation:
13) Direct the Municipal
Treasurer to immediately
liquidate cash advances as
soon as the purpose has
been served;

15) Submit to the Office of the
Auditor the liquidation
reports within ten (10) days
after the ensuing month of
the transactions.

16) We recommended that
Management direct the
concerned officials to
furnish a copy of
contract/purchase order to
the Auditor and its
enumerated in COA
Circular No. 2009-001
within five (5) working days
after perfection/approval/execution
for proper review and
evaluation.

21) We recommended that
Management, particularly
the members of the BAC, to
strictly comply with the
aforesaid regulations. In
case/s of procurement
method applied not within
the approved Annual
Procurement Plan, the BAC
through a resolution must
recommend to the Head of
Procuring Agency the
specific alternative method
of procurement to be used
and secure the prior
approval, before resorting to
any alternative method of
procurement pursuant to
Sec. 48.1 and 48.3 of the
Revised IRR of RA 9184.

1.8 We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Inventory Committee to continue the conduct of
complete physical count on all types of PPE, and prepare the
prescribed annual RPCPPE thereon which shall be submitted to
the Accounting Office for reconciliation with the subsidiary
accounts and balances to ensure reliability of PPE account
balances; and
b. Henceforth, submit the RPCPPE for the year ended December 31,
2021 to the Audit Team for further review.

2.6 We recommended that Management enjoin the:
a. Municipal Accountant record all purchases of supplies and
materials following the perpetual inventory method and an
inventory control account should be maintained for each inventory
item pursuant to Section 114 of the Manual on NGAS, Volume I; and
b. The Municipal GSO to maintain stock cards, consolidate weekly the
RIS using the SSMI to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant,
who shall compute the cost of supplies issued and ending inventory
using the moving average method as provided under Section 115 of
the same mentioned manual and prepare JEV to record the
appropriate expense accounts.

3.5 We recommended that Management ensure that a copy of contract
(such as MOA, purchase orders and its pertinent supporting
documents) be submitted for contract review within the prescribed five
(5)-day period from the execution / issuance of the contract pursuant to
COA Circular 2009-001.

4.7 We recommended that Management ensure due notification be made
pertaining to the time and date of every scheduled delivery of goods,
materials and services to the Auditor’s Office regularly pursuant to
Section 6.6 of COA Circular No. 95-006.

5.4 We recommended that Management:
a. Require the MPDO to submit annually (at the beginning of the
year) a list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to
be implemented during the year which shall include the project
name, implementing unit, office or division if not the agency as a
whole, brief description of the PPA, contractor or supplier, if any,
mode of procurement, funding source, cost or approved budget,
project duration including start and completion dates, and
location in compliance with item 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-
004;
b. Inform the Office of the SA and/or the ATL within ten (10) days
after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of
the program/activity that appropriate project signboards and/or
public notices are already posted pursuant to item 3.1 of the same
COA Circular; and
c. Submit to the ATL monthly monitoring report of the PPAs and
faithfully follow the provisions COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated
January 30, 2013, on the Information and Publicity of PPAs of
Government Agencies.

6.4 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant
and MDRRMO to prepare and submit the Report on LDRRMF every
month, whether there is transaction or utilization thereof or none for
the period, as required by COA Circular No. 2012-002.

7.7 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal
Accountant immediately comply with the requirements set forth under
Section 100 of PD No. 1445 relative to the timely submission of financial
records, reports and disbursement vouchers in order to have a reliable
basis in the verification and analysis of the financial transactions of the
agency.
1) Create an Inventory Team to conduct physical count of PPE and submit to the Municipal Accountant the result/report hereof, copy furnish the Auditor; and

2) Direct the GSO and
Municipal Accountant to
faithfully maintain
Property Cards and PPELC
for proper recording and
monitoring of properties of
the LGU.

3) Direct the MDRRMO, LDC, and Accounting Office to immediately furnish the Audit Team the following: Local Sanggunian Resolution on the use of QRF, LDRRMF Investment Plan and its supplemental plan,
Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF
and Utilization Report of Transferred Funds from the
Granter LGU; and

4) Direct the Accounting and
Budget Officer to maintain
registries for recording and
ultimately in the
presentation of
corresponding expense
accounts in the Financial
Statements.

5) We recommended that the
Management immediately
furnish the Audit Team the
updated PPAs, approved
Annual Investment Plan
and its Supplemental
Investment Program
embodying Covid-19
related expenses.

6) We recommended that the
Honorable Municipal Mayor direct the Local
Finance Committee, consisting of the Municipal
Budget Officer, the Municipal Treasurer and
the MPDO to submit the Approved Local
Ordinance/ Supplemental Budget for the BGCM
Fund and Monthly Report on Fund Utilization and
Status of PPAs Implementation and copy of posting of reports and
written notice and comply with the maintenance of
records for the accounting and budgeting of the said fund.
A. Non-submission of Supplemental Budget through an approved ordinance by the Local Sanggunian to support the legality of separate disbursement of BGCM Fund.
B.Non-conformity with the posting reporting requirements on the utilization and status report of BGCM is contrary to Sections 4.2 to 4.4 of Local Budget Circular No. 125 dated April 7, 2020.
C.Separate records like subsidiary ledgers, trial balances and proper accounting was not maintained by the Municipal Accountant as provided in Section 3.7 of LBC No. 125 dated April 7, 2020.

7) Faithfully comply with the
provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated
February 10, 1997 and the
existing rules and
regulations on the proper
cash management and
reporting;

A. The Cashbook-Cash in
Bank and Cashbook-
Cash Advances were not
properly filled in and no
reconciliation with the
Accounting records were
made at least quarterly.
8) Direct the Treasurer to
complete the details in
recording transactions in
Cashbooks; and

B. Some checks were
issued and recorded not
in numerical sequence or
chronologically order in
the Cashbook-Cash in
Bank indicating weak
internal control on cash
management.
9) Establish a sound internal
control by issuing checks
and recording them in
chronological sequence in
the Report of Checks
Issued and Cashbooks in
accordance to Section 42,
Volume I of the NGAS
Manual for LGUs.
10) Create an Inventory Team composed of at least three (3) members;
11) Direct the said Committee to conduct the annual physical count immediately and prepare the RPCPPE, to be reconciled with the books of accounts, and

12) Require the GSO and
Accounting Office to maintain
the appropriate
property cards and ledger
cards to establish the
correctness and existence
of the reported balances in
the financial statements.

14) Direct the Accountant to
refrain from granting cash
advances without full
liquidation of the previous
cash advances granted, and

17) Direct the Municipal
Treasurer, Accountant, Budget Officer, MPDC,
GSO and GAD Focal
Person, to immediately
comply and submit copies
of deficiencies noted in the
Validation Checklist to the
Office of the Team Leader
for review, and

18) Direct the same to faithfully
accomplish and maintain
financial records of all
transactions every month.

19) Re-evaluate policy on the
hiring of contractual
worker; and

20) Reassess capability of the
existing human resource;

22) Mobilize additional
competent personnel to
facilitate the inventory of all
PPE items;

24) Strengthen the controls on
the procedures of PPE
handling and recording by
performing a periodic
reconciliation between the
records of the accounting
office and the actual
property and inventory
results.

We recommended that Management direct the concerned officials to furnish a copy of contract/purchase order to the Auditor and its supporting documents as enumerated in COA Circular No. 2009-001 within 5 working days after perfection/approval/execution for proper review and auditorial and technical review of documents.

We recommended that Management, particularly the members of the BAC, to strictly comply with the aforesaid regulations. In case/s of procurement method applied not within the approved APP, the BAC through a resolution must recommend to the Head of Procuring Agency the specific alternative method of procurement to be used and secure the prior approval, before resorting to any alternative method of procurement pursuant to Sec. 48.1 and 48.3 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

1.6 We recommended that the Honorable Municipal Mayor:
a. Create an Inventory Team to conduct physical count of PPE and
submit to the Municipal Accountant the result/report hereof, copy
furnish the Auditor; and
b. Direct the GSO and Municipal Accountant to faithfully maintain
Property Cards and PPELC for proper recording and monitoring
of properties of the LGU.

2.9 We recommended that the Honorable Municipal Mayor implement
the following:
a. Direct the Municipal DRRMO, LDC, and Accounting Office to
immediately furnish the Audit Team the following: Local
Sanggunian Resolution on the use of QRF, LDRRMF Investment
Plan and its supplemental plan, Monthly Report on Sources and
Utilization of DRRMF and Utilization Report of Transferred
Funds from the Granter LGU; and
b. Direct the Accounting and Budget Officer to maintain registries
for recording and ultimately in the presentation of corresponding
expense accounts in the Financial Statements

3.5 We recommended that the Management immediately furnish the
Audit Team the updated Programs, Projects and Activities, approved
Annual Investment Plan and its Supplemental Investment Program
embodying Covid-19 related expenses.

4.13 We recommended that the Honorable Municipal Mayor direct the
Local Finance Committee, consisting of the Municipal Budget Officer,
the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Planning and
Development Officer (MPDO) to submit the Approved Local
Ordinance/Supplemental Budget for the BGCM Fund and Monthly
Report on Fund Utilization and Status of Program/Project/Activity
Implementation and copy of posting of reports and written notice and
comply with the maintenance of separate records for the accounting
and budgeting of the said fund.

5.10 We recommended that Management implement the following:
a. Faithfully comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002
dated February 10, 1997 and the existing rules and regulations on
the proper cash management and reporting;
b. Direct the Treasurer to complete the details in recording
transactions in the Cashbooks; and
c. Establish a sound internal control by issuing checks and recording
them in chronological sequence in the Report of Checks Issued
and Cashbooks in accordance to Section 42, Volume I of the
NGAS Manual for LGUs.

6.7 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal
Accountant immediately comply with the requirements set forth
under Section 100 of P.D. No. 1445 relative to the timely submission of
financial records, reports and disbursement vouchers in order to have
a reliable basis in the verification and analysis of the financial
transactions of the agency.

We recommended the Management to direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer prepare and submit the List of PPAs for CY 2019 to COA and its quarterly monitoring report, and to faithfully observe the LGU’s guidelines set forth by the Commission to promote transparency and encourage public participation and right to information.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to submit the Monthly Reports on the Receipt and Utilization of the DRRMF from January to December 2019 and the LDRRMF Investment Plan, in support to the disbursements made from the same.

We recommended that Management adhere to the mandate of RA 9710(Magna Carta for Women) and prepare the GAD Plan and Budget duly endorsed by the DILG; and ensure the effective implementation of GAD PPAs to be reflected in GAD Accomplishment Report, copy furnish the Auditor.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive: 14. Direct the Municipal Treasurer, Accountant, Budget Officer, MPDC, GSO and GAD Focal Person, to immediately comply and submit copies of deficiencies noted in the Validation Checklist to the Office of the Team Leader for review, and
15. Direct the same to faithfully accomplish and maintain financial records of all transactions every month.
We recommended that Management submit to the auditor concerned all documents pertaining to the loan as soon as possible for proper evaluation and examination.

We recommended that the Management:
17. Re-evaluate policy on the hiring of contractual worker;
18. Reassess capability of the existing human resource; and
19. Discontinue contracts of service which might be found unnecessary, and strictly comply with the requisites of law on the procurement of manpower.

We recommended that Management direct the concerned officials to submit copies of Purchase Orders/Contracts and AIR on time to the Auditor’s Office and notify the said office of the delivery of procured items within 24 hours from such acceptance of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries as required under Section A (1-2) of COA Circular No. 96-010 and Section 118 of NGAS.

We recommend that Management direct the Accounting Office or the Inventory Committee to:
22. Mobilize additional competent personnel to facilitate the inventory of all property;
23. Prepare and maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards; and
24. Strengthen the controls on the procedures of PPE handling and recording by performing a periodic reconciliation between the records of the accounting office and the actual property and inventory results.

We recommended that Management:
27. Direct the Board for the preparation of the SWMP which should be reviewed and updated as required by law to ensure its sustainability, relevance and effectiveness; and
28. Collaborate the campaign down to the barangay officials concerned.

29. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all concerned officials to prepare all necessary records pertaining to the Trust Fund and the necessary adjustments for the transfer of PPE Accounts to the General Fund books.
30. Prepare and submit Report of Collection and Deposit with all its supporting documents within the reglementary period set by rules.
1. We recommended that Management submit to the auditor concerned all documents pertaining to the loan as soon as possible for proper evaluation and examination.

11.Mobilize additional competent personnel to facilitate the inventory of all; 12.Classify properly PPE items; 13.Prepare and maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, and 14.Strengthen the controls on the procedures of PPE handling and recording by performing a periodic reconciliation between the records of the accounting office and the actual property and inventory results.

16.Direct the Municipal Accountant to get the monthly bank statements promptly and regularly; 17.Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the Bank Reconciliation Statements of all bank accounts together with the bank statements and paid checks to the auditor, and 18.Direct the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile his cashbooks entries with that from the accounting records at least quarterly.
observations.reason: No competent personnel to handle the job.

observations.recommendation: 24.We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all concerned officials to prepare all necessary records pertaining to the Trust Fund and the necessary adjustments for the transfer of PPE Accounts to the General Fund books.

1.6 We recommended that Local Chief Executive:
a) Create an Inventory Team composed of at least three (3)
members;
b) Direct the said Committee to conduct the annual physical count
immediately and prepare the RPCPPE, to be reconciled with the
books of accounts, and
c) Require the GSO and Municipal Accountant to maintain the
appropriate property cards and ledger cards to establish the
correctness and existence of the reported balances in the
financial statements.

2.3 We recommended that Local Chief Executive:
a) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately liquidate cash
advances as soon as the purpose has been served;
b) Direct the Accountant to refrain from granting cash advances
without full liquidation of the previous cash advances granted,
and
c) Submit to the Office of the Auditor the liquidation reports within
ten (10) days after the ensuing month of the transactions.

3.7 We recommended that Local Chief Executive:
a) Utilize the 20% DF in conformity with the priority
projects/programs in the Annual Investment Plan;
b) Direct the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator (MPDC) to review unimplemented
PPAs and determine if it is still a vital need for development and
realign/reprogram to an identified priority PPA, and
c) Direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit to the
auditor the supporting documents of all loan payments.

4.4 We recommended that Management to direct the MPDC and the
Municipal Engineer prepare and submit the List of PPAs for CY 2019 to
COA and its quarterly monitoring report, and to faithfully observe the
guidelines set forth by the Commission to promote good governance,
encourage public participation and right to information.

5.5 We recommended that Management direct the concerned officials to
furnish a copy of contract/purchase order to the Auditor and its
supporting documents as enumerated in COA Circular No. 2009-001
within five (5) working days after perfection/approval/execution for
proper review and evaluation.

6.7 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant
to submit the Monthly Reports on the Receipt and Utilization of the
DRRMF from January to December 2019 and the LDRRMF Investment
Plan, in support to the disbursements made from the same.

7.7 We strongly recommended that Local Chief Executive:
a) Direct the Municipal Treasurer, Accountant, Budget Officer,
MPDC, GSO and GAD Focal Person, to immediately comply and
submit copies of deficiencies noted in the Validation Checklist to
the Office of the Team Leader for review, and
b) Direct the same to faithfully accomplish and maintain financial
records of all transactions every month.

8.5 We recommended that Local Chief Executive adhere to the mandate of
RA 9710 (Magna Carta for Women) and prepare the GAD Plan and
Budget duly endorsed by the DILG; and ensure the effective
implementation of GAD PPAs to be reflected in GAD Accomplishment
Report, copy furnish the Auditor.

2. We recommended that Management require the Municipal DRRMO to prepare and submit to the Sangguniang Bayan, through the MDRRM Council, the annual LDRRMFIP which shall present the 30% allocation for Quick Response Fund in lump-sum and the 70% allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness the details as to projects and activities to be funded pursuant to Section 112 (c) of the PDRRMA and COA Circular 2012-002.

3. Re-evaluate policy on the hiring of contractual worker; 4. Reassess capability of the existing human resource, and 5. Discontinue contracts of service, which might be found unnecessary, and strictly comply with the requisites of law on the procurement of manpower.

6. We recommended that Management, particularly the members of the BAC, to strictly comply with the aforesaid regulations. In case/s of procurement method applied not within the approved APP, the BAC through a resolution must recommend to the Head of Procuring Agency the specific alternative method of procurement to be used and secure the prior approval, before resorting to any alternative method of procurement pursuant to Sec. 48.1 and 48.3 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

7. We recommended that Management continue promoting a gender responsive governance to comply with the government’s objective of promoting and protecting women’s human rights and economic empowerment and to pass a GAD Code to easily determine the PAPs that addresses gender issues and concerns in compliance with Section 37B Rule VI of Republic Act 9710.

8. We recommended that Management require the Accountant, the Treasurer and other concerned officials to strictly comply with the reporting procedures and deadline of submission to the Auditor’s Office.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant still incurs delays in submitting financial reports.

observations.recommendation: 9. We recommended that Management identify projects and programs under this fund in accordance with the provision of the DILG and DBM Joint Circular No. 1 Series of 2005, to provide desirable socio-economic development which are beneficial to the constituents and direct the concerned officials to submit the complete required documents necessary for early audit.

10.We recommended that the Management maximize the utilization of the Materials Recovery Facility (MRF). We also recommended that management assist the barangays technically or in any other manner deemed necessary in order to sustain the operation of the MRFs.

15.We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to record purchases of supplies and the like as Inventory Account and record the related expenses account upon issuance of inventories based on RSMI.

19.Allocate for 5% Calamity Fund which should be transferred to a separate account and utilized solely for LDRRMF purpose activities; 20.Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit report on the sources and utilization of DRRMF on a monthly basis, and 21.Prepare and submit Annual DRRM Program with specifics for the 70% and 30% QRF.

22.Direct the Board for the preparation of the SWMP which should be reviewed and updated as required by law to ensure its sustainability, relevance and effectiveness, and 23.Collaborate the campaign down to the barangay officials concerned.
25.We recommended that the Management allocate 1% of IRA for the establishment of LCPC and implementation of their programs.

26.Maintain an updated cashbook on a daily basis, by fund and/or by account, and 27.Prepare and submit Report of Collection and Deposit (RCD), with all its supporting documents within the reglementary period set by rules.
observations.reason: Management has still not submitted the RCDs as of report date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management allocate one percent (1%) of IRA for the establishment of the Local Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC) and the implementation of their programs.
observations.managementAction: Agency head order Accountable responsible officials to comply

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: 1. Allocate for 5% Calamity Fund which should be transferred to a separate account special trust fund and utilized solely for LDRRMF purpose activities. 2. Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit report on the sources and utilization of DRRMF on a monthly basis. 3. Prepare and submit Annual DRRM program with specific for the seventy percent (70%) preparedness and thirty percent (30%) quick response.
observations.managementAction: Agency head order Accountable responsible officials to comply

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Direct the Board for the preparation of the Solid Waste Management Plan which should be reviewed and updated as required by law to ensure its sustainability, relevance and effectiveness; b. Collaborate the campaign down to the barangay officials concerned;

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all concerned officials to prepare all necessary records pertaining to the Trust Fund and the necessary adjustments for the transfer of Property, Plant and Equipment accounts from Trust Fund books to General Fund books.

We recommended that the Management widen the allocation of utilization of 20% development fund to the three priority programs and projects such as social development, economic development and environment management.

1.4. We recommended that Management submit to the auditor concerned all
documents pertaining to the loan as soon as possible for proper evaluation
and examination.

2.5. We recommended that Management require the Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer to prepare and submit to the
Sangguniang Bayan, through the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction
Management Council (MDRRMC), the annual Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) which
shall present the 30 percent allocation for Quick Response Fund in lump-sum
and the 70 percent allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and
preparedness with details as to projects and activities to be funded pursuant
to Section 12 (c) of the PDRRMA and COA Circular No. 2012-002.

3.6. We recommended that Management:
a. Re-evaluate policy on the hiring of contractual worker.
b. Reassess capability of the existing human resource.
c. Discontinue contracts of service which might be found unnecessary, and
strictly comply with the requisites of law on the procurement of manpower.

4.5. We recommended that the Management particularly the members of the
Bids and Award Committee, strictly comply with the aforesaid regulations.
In case/s of procurement method applied not within the approved APP, the
BAC through a resolution must recommend to the Head of Procuring Agency
the specific alternative method of procurement to be used and secure the
prior approval, before resorting to any alternative method of procurement
pursuant to Sec. 48.1 and 48.3 of the revised IRR of RA 9184.

5.4. We recommended that Management:
a. Submit to the Audit Team the list of all on-going government
Programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs) and those that are to be
implemented during the year.
b. Inform this office within ten (10) days after the award of the
infrastructure project or before the start of the Programs/Projects/
Activities that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices
are already posted and.
c. Submit to this Office the monthly monitoring report on PPAs.

6.4. We recommended that Management direct the concerned officials to submit
copies of Purchase Orders/Contracts and Acceptance and Inspection Reports
on time to the Auditor’s Office and notify the said office of the delivery of
procured items within twenty-four (24) hours from such acceptance of the
time and date of the scheduled deliveries as required under Section A (1-2) of
COA Circular No. 96-010 and Section 118 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.

7.5. We recommended that Management continue promoting a gender responsive
governance to comply with the government’s objective of promoting and
protecting women’s human rights and economic empowerment and to pass a
GAD Code to easily determine the PAPs that addresses gender issues and
concerns in compliance with Section 37B Rule VI of Republic Act No. 9710.

8.4. We recommended that Management require the accountant, the Treasurer
and other concerned officials to strictly comply with the reporting
procedures and deadlines of the submission to the Auditor office.

9.4. We recommended that the Management identify projects and programs
under this fund in accordance with the provision of the DILG and DBM Joint
circular No. 1 series of 2005, to provide desirable socio-economic
development which are beneficial to the constituents and direct the
concerned officials to submit the complete required documents necessary for
audit.

10.3. We recommended that the Management maximize the utilization of the
Materials Recovery Facility (MRF). We also recommend that management
assist the barangays technically or in any other manner deemed necessary in
order to sustain the operation of the MRFs.

12.5. We recommended that Management require the supply officer to record
purchases of supplies and the like as Inventory account and record the
related expenses account upon issuance of inventories bases on RSMI.

We have recommended Management: 1. Direct the Municipal Accountant to get the monthly bank statements promptly and regularly. 2. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit Bank Reconciliation Statements of all bank accounts together with the bank statements and paid checks to the Auditor. 3. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile his cashbooks entries with that from the accounting records at least quarterly.
observations.reason: No competent personnel to handle the job.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer to; 1. Maintain an updated cashbook on a daily basis, by fund and or by account. 2. Prepare and submit Report of Collection and Deposits (RCD), with all its supporting documents within the reglementary period set by rules.

We recommended the following measures, to wit; a. If no records could be found, start recording the actual existence of equipment, furniture and fixtures by the pertinent office and if possible reconstruct the records. b. Instruct the General Services Officer to provide property number of all properties, at the same time, maintain stock cards with the Municipal Accountant who will maintain a subsidiary ledger card. c. The Municipal Accountant should prepare necessary adjustments on the deficiencies noted in the course of inventory taking and make reconciliation with inventory report, stock cards and subsidiary ledgers.
We recommend that Management conduct inventory of its road networks system and record in the books of accounts its Local Road Network in the Books of Accounts.
observations.managementAction: Agency head order Accountable responsible officials to comply

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LCE require the concerned official or the Municipal Assessor to give emphasis on the collection of taxes and or delinquent real property taxes by applying the remedies in the collection of delinquent taxes as embodied in Section 254 of R.A. No. 7160 in order to set up in the books the estimated real property tax collectibles and the special education fund share.
observations.managementAction: Agency head order Accountable responsible officials to comply

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: 1. Create Comprehensive Land Use Plan (CLUP) 2. Develop Long Term LDRRMF Plan which will serve as guide in building the Agency calamity preparedness and adaptive capacity in terms of disasters. 3. Establish Incident Command System (ICS) to ensure effective management of consequences of disaster as explicitly provided in R.A. No. 10121.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the Management:

        1.  Direct  the  Municipal  Accountant  to  get  the  monthly  bank
           statements promptly and regularly.


        2. Require the Municipal Accountant  to  prepare  and  submit  Bank
           Reconciliation Statements of all bank accounts together with the
           bank statements and paid checks to the Auditor


        3. Direct  the  Municipal  Treasurer  to  reconcile  his  cashbooks
           entries  with  that  from  the  accounting  records   at   least
           quarterly.

We recommended the following measures, to wit;


     a. If no records could be found, start recording the actual  existence of equipment, furniture and fixtures  by  office  and  if  possible reconstruct the records.


     b. Instruct the General Services Officer to provide property number of all properties, at the same time, maintain  stock  cards  with  the Municipal Accountant who will maintain a subsidiary ledger card.




     c. The Municipal Accountant should prepare  necessary  adjustments  on the deficiencies noted in the course of inventory taking  and  make reconciliation with inventory report, stock  cards  and  subsidiary ledgers.

We recommended that  Management conduct inventory of its road networks system and record in the books of accounts its  equivalent  amount  pursuant to item IX of COA Circular 2015-008 date November 23, 2015.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE)  require  the  concerned official or the Municipal Assessor to give emphasis  on  the  collection  of taxes and or delinquent real property taxes by applying the remedies in  the  collection of delinquent taxes as embodied in Section 254 of R.A. No.  7160 in  order  to  set  up  in  the  books  the  estimated  real  property   tax collectibles and the special education fund share.
We recommended that the Management allocate one percent (1%) of IRA for  the establishment of Local Council for the Protection  of  Children  (LCPC)  and the implementation of their programs.

We recommended that Management:


     1.    Create Comprehensive Land Use Plan (CLUP)


     2.    Develop Long Term LDRRMF Plan  which  will  serve  as  guide  in
        building the calamity preparedness and adaptive capacity  in  terms
        of       disasters.




     3.    Establish Incident Command  System  (ICS)  to  ensure  effective
        consequences management of disaster as explicitly provided in  R.A.
        No.      10121.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive

     1. Allocate for 5% Calamity Fund which  should  be  transferred  to  a separate account special trust fund and utilized solely for  LDRRMF purpose activities


     2. Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit report on the sources and utilization of DRRMF on a monthly basis.


     3. Prepare and submit  Annual  DRRM  program  with  specific  for  the seventy percent (70%) preparedness and thirty percent  (30%)  quick response.

We  recommended  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  direct  all   concerned officials to prepare all necessary records pertaining to the Trust Fund  and the necessary adjustments for the transfer of Property, Plant and  Equipment accounts from Trust Fund books to General Fund books.

We recommended that the Management widen the allocation  of  utilization  of 20% development fund to the three priority programs  and projects such as social development, economic development and environment management.

We recommend that the
management observe/adhere
to the guidelines
stipulated in COA
Circular No. 2013-004
dated January 30, 2013
RE: Information and
Publicity of Programs,
Projects and Activities
of government agencies.
This is to promote Good
Governance through
transparency and
accountability.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the management observe adhere to the guidelines stipulated in COA
Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 RE: Information and Publicity of Programs,
Projects and Actwvities of government agencies. This is to promote Good Governance through
transparency and accountability.

1. Strict compliance with the Laws, Rules and Regulations of LDRRMF implementation is
hereby required, and Submit Accomplishment Report thereof to the Auditor concerned Jfor
proper evaluation with the breakdown of expenditure and or list of LDRRMF related
respects of activities.

2. The Municipal Accountant should set aside a subsidy to other funds and transfer prior
Vyears unexpended and or unobligated balance of the LDRRMF equivalent to 30% from the
Creneral Fund to the Trust Book using Trust Liability - DRRM.

3. The Municipal Budget Officer should likewise maintain subsidiary ledger and records of
the appropriation corresponding expenditures and balances of the LDRRMF for the current
and previous years.

1. Management should formulate Plans and Programs for GAD activities.
2. Management should submit GAD Accomplishment Report to the auditor concerned.

It is recommend that the management should widen the allocation of utilization of 20%
development fund to the three priority programs and projects such as social development,
economic development and environment management.

*Prioritize the iitiation of physical count on all PPE as this will heip concerned officials in
the proper discharge of their duties and responsibilities towards fair presentation of the
agency's financial statements.

* Create an Inventory Committee to take charge in the overall physical count on PPE and
submit corresponding reports to the office of the auditor immediately thereafter.

* The Accounting office and General Services office/Treasurer office shall likewise, maintain
and update an Equipment Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively and oftentimes
reconcile balances of each cards maintained by them and with the balances in the Report on
Physical Count to be able to reflect complete and accurate PPE balance in the financial
statements.

* Provision for depreciation shall be accurately provided on all depreciable assets.

1t is recommended that the top management should mitiate necessary reforms for fiscal reporting
and accountability by:

a. Demonstrating leadership by example;

b, Training personnel to gain work competence and'or hiring additional qualified personnel
and or restricting existing personnel giving the right job for the right person; and

¢ Enforcing due compliance with laws, rules and regulations on the rendition of financial
reports.

It is further recommended that the top management should direct the concerned municipal
accountable officer to submut promptly the period financial reports together with the pertinent
supporting documents to the concerned local auditor so that timely post-audit of transactions
could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated. Consequently, the top
management could also be informed immediately of any deficiencies’errors detected and
corrections/adjustments thereof could be affected to their advantage.

Require the concerned Municipal Accountant to observe the keeping of accounts and
subsidiary records of all accounts in compliance with Section 11 and 114 (2) of Presidential
Decree No. 1445,

Encourage and urge the Local Chief Executive to establish a sound ecological solid
waste management system and constitute the Municipality Solid Waste Management (SWM)
Boards in pursuant to Section 2 (par. 2), Rule VIII and Section 1, Rule V1 of IRR of RA 5447.
